2605.01386.title: MemORAI: Memory Organization and Retrieval via Adaptive Graph Intelligence for LLM Conversational Agents
2605.01386.paper_summary: Large Language Models (LLMs) lack persistent memory for long-term personalized conversations. Existing graph-based memory systems suffer from information dilution, absent provenance tracking, and uniform retrieval that ignores query context. We introduce MemORAI (Memory Organization and Retrieval via Adaptive Graph Intelligence), a framework that integrates three innovations: selective memory filtering with dual-layer compression to retain user-persona-relevant content, a provenance-enriched multi-relational graph tracking factual origins at the turn level, and query-adaptive subgraph retrieval with Dynamic Weighted PageRank that applies query-conditioned edge weighting. Evaluated on LOCOMO and LongMemEval benchmarks, MemORAI achieves state-of-the-art performance in memory retrieval and personalized response generation, demonstrating that selective storage, enriched representation, and adaptive retrieval are essential for coherent, personalized LLM agents.
2605.01386.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）缺乏持久的記憶以進行長期的個性化對話。現有的基於圖形的記憶系統存在信息稀釋、缺乏來源追蹤以及忽略查詢上下文的統一檢索等問題。我們介紹了MemORAI（通過自適應圖智能進行的記憶組織和檢索），這是一個整合了三項創新的框架：選擇性記憶過濾，配合雙層壓縮以保留與用戶角色相關的內容；一個豐富來源的多關係圖，在轉換層級追蹤事實來源；以及查詢自適應子圖檢索，使用動態加權PageRank，應用查詢條件邊權重。在LOCOMO和LongMemEval基準測試中進行評估，MemORAI在記憶檢索和個性化回應生成方面達到了最先進的性能，證明了選擇性存儲、豐富表示和自適應檢索對於連貫的個性化LLM代理是至關重要的。
2605.01386.author: Hung Pham Van et.al.
2605.01386.authors: Hung Pham Van, Nguyen Manh Hieu, Khang Pham Tran Tuan, Nam Le Hai, Linh Ngo Van, Nguyen Thi Ngoc Diep, Trung Le
